t_ch: 想这些也没用，还是做好手头的每一件事吧。
t_en: Thinking about these things is pointless. I just have to make sure I do everything properly.

t_ch: 很多人都称赞我的努力。但他们不知道…那只是因为我一无所有。
t_en: Many people praise my hard work. But, what they don't know is... it is all because I have nothing.

t_ch: 我拼命学习，是为了折纸大学的奖学金，为了那份荣誉…为了找到一份好工作……
t_en: All of my hard work studying is so I can attain the Paperfold University Scholarship. For that honor... And for finding a good job...

t_ch: 只有这样，我才能留在这里，从无数学生中脱颖而出，有机会实现自己的梦想。
t_en: That is the only way that I will be able to stay here, stand out amongst the countless other students, and have a chance to achieve my dreams.

t_ch: 没什么问题。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 你已经做的很好了。
t_en: You have done extremely well.

t_ch: 但…这里是匹诺康尼，梦境里的人们大多身世显赫，对地位与价值更在乎，也更敏感。
t_en: But... this is Penacony. The majority of people from the Dreamscape are from notable backgrounds and are far more interested in and wary of status and value.

t_ch: 比如老是来烦我的那家伙，要不是因为惹到他，恐怕他绝对不会和我有任何交集。
t_en: It's like that guy who is always bothering me. If I hadn't angered him, I fear he never would have bothered interacting with me at all.

t_ch: 老实说，那家伙也有点可取之处。对那些从最开始就拥有许多东西的人来说，为了证明自己而不懈努力，这也是个挺难能可贵的品质。
t_en: If I'm honest, there are things I could learn from that guy. For someone like him who has always been blessed with abundance from birth, that attitude of always working hard to prove oneself is actually a very precious quality.

t_ch: 所以偶尔我会想，我和他…是否有共同前行的可能？
t_en: So I sometimes think to myself... maybe he and I could find a common thread?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你别当真，只是无谓的思绪罢了。不管再怎么努力，我始终…不属于那个世界。
t_en: Hahaha, ignore me. They're just pointless thoughts... No matter how hard I work, I... I will never belong to that world.

t_ch: 那些没有职业素养的人…真让人作呕。
t_en: Those who lack professionalism... They make me sick.

t_ch: 那些人究竟什么时候才能意识到——筑梦在成为艺术前，首先是一项工程！
t_en: When will these people realize that... before dreamweaving became an art, it was a form of engineering!

t_ch: 我赞同阿锡卡教授的看法。无论外观如何繁杂，最基础的功能性都不可或缺。
t_en: I agree with Professor Asika's views. It does not matter how elaborate the exterior appearance is. A construction must at least serve its most basic functions.

t_ch: 那些为了好看，用发光忆质建造住宅的人，就该让他们自己住进去，等被天花板晃得睁不开眼的时候，看他们怎么后悔！
t_en: Those people who use sparkling memoria to construct residential buildings should have to move into them themselves. They'll regret their choices when the ceilings end up blinding them!

t_ch: 呼…这种人还只是蠢，已经算好的了。有另一种是坏——
t_en: Phew... These people are just plain stupid, which is almost a relief. It's the truly malevolent ones we need to worry about...

t_ch: 为了降低成本而使用薄弱忆质？提前结束工期，放着空荡荡的屋顶不管？为了钱，这些家伙什么事都干得出来。
t_en: Using low-quality memoria to lower costs? Ending construction projects early and leaving roofs wide open? These people will do anything for money.

t_ch: 要是在现实里，这就是…谋杀！就是因为有这种渣滓存在，我的家人才会……
t_en: If this was real life, this would simply be... murder! It's because of scum like them that my family...

t_ch: 我发誓，这样的败类，见一个我就举报一个！
t_en: I vow that I will personally report each and every one of these degenerates!

t_ch: …在我看来，这不单单是对教师们正当权益的侵犯，更是对学生的不负责！故而我的诉求是……
t_en: ...In my opinion, this is not only a violation of the legitimate rights and interests of teaching staff, it is also a disservice to the students! Therefore, my request is...

t_ch: 唔…刚刚那句措辞似乎不够严厉…换成「拿学生们的前途开玩笑」或许更适合些？
t_en: Hmm... I don't feel like that phrasing is harsh enough... Maybe I should change it to: "making a joke of student futures"?

t_ch: 所以你的诉求是？
t_en: So, what is your request?

t_ch: 说话别说一半啊！
t_en: Don't mince your words!

t_ch: …嗯？偷听得如此理直气壮，你也是个人才。所以这位同学，你找我有事吗？
t_en: ...Hm? Being able to eavesdrop and still seem so self-righteous is a talent. Was there something you wanted to talk to me about?

t_ch: 我只是个路过的普通学生。
t_en: I'm just a passing student.

t_ch: 路过都不忘吃口瓜是吧。不过…要真这么好奇的话，和你说说也不是不行。
t_en: Just making sure to get the gossip as you wander by, huh? Although... I guess I could tell you more if you're really curious.

t_ch: 我很好奇，要不你继续？
t_en: I'm curious, please go on?

t_ch: 行啊，稍微满足下你的好奇心也无妨…毕竟这东西就像脑子，有总比没有强。
t_en: Alright, I guess I could satisfy your curiosity... Curiosity is like a brain — it's always better to have it.

t_ch: 简而言之，前些天我从学生那里听说，谐乐学院有位负责梦泡鉴赏课程的老教授，对众多星球的文化根本一无所知。
t_en: To put it simply, I recently heard from a student that there is a professor working in the Charmony Academy dream bubble connoisseurship course who knows absolutely nothing about interplanetary culture.

t_ch: 对此我大为震撼，甚至装作学生去听了他好几堂课，结果嘛……
t_en: I was absolutely shocked! I ended up actually going to a few of his classes and, well...

t_ch: 你猜怎么着，那个老教授不仅连几艘仙舟的名字都讲不出来，把天击将军说成是步离人，还宣称《银河忍法帖》的那个主角是从出云来的。
t_en: Guess what? Not only could he not even name the few Xianzhou ships, but he also said that the Merlin's Claw is a borisin and that the main character in the Cosmic Ninjutsu Inscriptions is from Izumo!

t_ch: 学院的资深教授，上课讲的内容却漏洞百出，你说好不好笑？
t_en: He's an experienced academy professor, yet his teachings were full of holes. Isn't that ridiculous?

t_ch: …有点离谱。
t_en: ...That's outrageous.

t_ch: 是吧。这种老师还能留在折纸大学任教，简直离谱！
t_en: Right? It's outrageous that a teacher like that would be able to stay and serve as a professor at Paperfold University!

t_ch: 这些知识连我都知道。
t_en: Even I know these things.

t_ch: 是吗？看来你的涉猎范围也很广啊，等以后有空，我们来好好交流下。
t_en: Is that so? I see you have a vast scope of research. We should find time for a good discussion.

t_ch: 更好笑的还在后面呢——我尝试通过各种渠道向校方反映这件事，最终全都石沉大海，连个正经的回应都没有。
t_en: That's not even the most ridiculous part... I have tried to report this to the university through various channels and yet it's been completely swept under the rug! They haven't even given me a decent reply!

t_ch: 无意自夸，但就专业水平而言，我们这些年轻教师完全不比这种上课只会念教材的老…咳，老前辈要差。
t_en: I don't mean to boast, but in terms of professional capability, we young teachers are just as good as these old... ahem, our seniors, who just spend all their time reading out from the textbook.

t_ch: 可现实就是如此无奈：有的人能凭资历尸位素餐，霸占教授的位置；而有的人满肚子真才实学，却只能开个选修课。
t_en: But, there is no changing the reality: Some people manage to occupy the professor positions based purely on their history and experience, while those who possess genuine talent and intellect are left with nothing but elective courses.

t_ch: 现在知道我的诉求了吧——机会难得，得趁这次校庆，向学院当面讨个说法才行。
t_en: Now you understand my complaint, right? Opportunities like this don't come around often. I will take the school anniversary celebration as my moment to bring this matter to the university face-to-face.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 还有问题？要是我的学生能像你这么积极就好了……
t_en: You have more questions? I wish my students were as interested and proactive as you...

t_ch: 再讲讲那个老教授吧。
t_en: Tell me more about this professor.

t_ch: 哈…其实他课讲得也没那么烂——不过是半个系统时内读错了五个音、误用了三次典故、还把两篇文献的作者完全混淆了而已。
t_en: Ah... If I'm honest, his lectures weren't really that bad... It's just that in half a system hour, he mispronounced five words, used three incorrect quotes, and completely confused the authors of two separate documents.

t_ch: 你知道当我听他信誓旦旦地把「翾翔」念成「寰翔」时的感受吗？
t_en: Do you know how I felt when he said "aeronautics" instead of "aerodynamics" with a completely straight face?

t_ch: 参加博识学会讲座的时候，就听说有位教授对白痴过敏…如今我可算知道那究竟是个什么滋味了！
t_en: When I attended a lecture at the Intelligentsia Guild, I heard that there was a professor who was allergic to stupidity... Now I know what that feels like!

t_ch: 学校不管吗？
t_en: Does the school not care?

t_ch: 理论上来说是该管。但实际上——你不能因为一所学校建在「美梦」里、并且以「美梦多彩，理想自由」为校训，就真指望它能「完美」得像个梦似的。
t_en: Theoretically, they should care about it. However, in reality... you cannot expect a school to achieve the "perfection" of a dream simply because it is built inside a dream and has the motto: "Color your dreams, free your ideals."

t_ch: 只要有人的地方就有利益纠葛，就有纷争，毕竟…哎，所有人都这样。
t_en: Wherever there are people, there will be conflicts of interest and disagreements. After all... that is just how people are.

t_ch: 不过当然，这并不影响我为自己和学生们的权益继续抗争下去。
t_en: However, naturally, that won't stop me from continuing to defend the rights of myself and my students.

t_ch: 你忙吧，不打扰了。
t_en: I'll let you get on.

t_ch: 听够了，满意了？那就去玩你自己的吧。
t_en: You've heard enough to satisfy you? Go and entertain yourself then.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. See ya.

t_ch: 唔，稿子刚才念到哪了来着……
t_en: Ah, where did I just read up to...

t_ch: 嗯？又是你啊……
t_en: Hm? You again...

t_ch: 既然这么喜欢跟我交流，不如下学期来选修我的课程吧？我打算讲「翁瓦克的民俗与文化」，一点都不难，还有挺多课外实践。
t_en: As you enjoy talking to me so much, how about taking my elective next semester? I plan to teach the customs and culture of Vonwacq. It's super easy and there will be loads of extracurricular activities.

t_ch: 而且…看在是熟人的份上，说不定期末考试的时候，我还能给你加几分呢？
t_en: Plus... as we're friends, I may be able to bump your grades up a bit when it comes to end-of-term exams?

t_ch: …归根结底，又有什么区别呢？
t_en: ...At the end of the day, what's the difference?

t_ch: 几年前，为了做田野调查，我曾到访过某个偏远贫瘠的星球。那里的人们还处于游牧与农耕的过渡期，比起「生活」，他们更加注重「生存」。
t_en: A few years ago, I once visited a remote and barren planet for my field research. The local people were still in the transitional period between nomadism and agriculture. They were more preoccupied with "survival" than "living."

t_ch: 我以高高在上的视角去评判那些当地居民：我看着他们整日躬耕不辍却收获寥寥，看着他们为猎物分配不均而大打出手，并为此生出虚伪的怜悯。
t_en: I looked down on them with a sense of superiority and judged the locals: I watched them toiling all day to receive meager harvests and get into disputes over the uneven distribution of game meat. I felt an almost hypocritical pity for them.

t_ch: 可如今我越发觉得，我们和他们、这里和那里，其实没有什么本质上的区分——
t_en: But now I realize, more and more, that there is no difference between us and them, between our planet and theirs...

t_ch: 我们这些所谓的*学者*啊，整日在知识的田野中躬耕不辍却收获寥寥，还得为了教学资源明争暗斗，恨不得大打出手。
t_en: We so-called scholars spend all day toiling in the fields of knowledge to come back with dry harvests. Then, we plot and scheme over academic resources and can't wait to get into disputes.

t_ch: 那么，我该怜悯自己吗——毕竟对于我们这样的普通人而言，这世上并不存在什么纯粹的、知识的乐土。
t_en: So, should I pity myself? For normal people like us, there is no such thing as a pure paradise of knowledge after all.

t_ch: 年轻的教授嘴角噙着微笑，眼睛里却燃烧着安静的怒火。
t_en: A smile hung on the young professor's face, but a quiet rage burned in his eyes.

t_ch: 那位教授…我稍稍调查了一下他的背景：匹诺康尼本地人，家境优渥；本校毕业生，成绩一般但有个好舅舅——据说是家族的某位大人物。
t_en: That professor... I did some secret research into his background: He's a Penacony native from a prestigious family. He graduated from this academy with average grades, but he has an uncle... who coincidentally is a big shot in The Family.

t_ch: 哈…为了研究我可以奔赴险恶的荒野，深入未知的雨林，可以不吃不喝好几天去观察去记录…可他呢？
t_en: Ah... For the sake of my research, I would be willing to traverse the dangerous wilds or go deep into unknown rainforests. I could go without food or water for many days to observe and record... But what about him?

t_ch: 纯粹的知识无法为他带来任何喜悦，只是他用以营生的工具、博得名利的踏板。
t_en: Pure knowledge brings him no joy. It is just a tool for him to make a living, a springboard for fame.

t_ch: …这个送给你。以后你可不能成为这样面目可憎的人啊。
t_en: ...I want you to have this. I hope that you will never become such a loathsome person.

t_ch: 那位教授…纯粹的知识无法为他带来任何喜悦，只是他用以营生的工具、博得名利的踏板。
t_en: That professor... Pure knowledge brings him no joy. It is just a tool for him to make a living, a springboard for fame.

t_ch: 就是这样的人…在我眼中格外面目可憎……
t_en: People like this... I find them especially loathsome...

t_ch: 真是精彩纷呈的一出大戏啊……
t_en: What a wonderful show...

t_ch: 不得不说，上次的报告会可真是太精彩了，我还是头一次看到三个学院的教授吵成那样——
t_en: I have to say, the last reporting session was truly wonderful. It was the first time I have ever seen professors from across the three academies argue so ferociously...

t_ch: 财富学院嘲笑筑梦学院「打灰的臭劳工」、筑梦学院说谐乐学院「假装清高」、谐乐学院批判财富学院「自我物化」……
t_en: The Fortune Academy lecturer called the Dreamweaver Academy lecturer "a stinky cement-mixing laborer." The Dreamweaver Academy lecturer said the Charmony Academy lecturer "puts on an act of superiority." The Charmony Academy lecturer criticized the Fortune Academy lecturer for "self-objectification"...

t_ch: 太精彩了…哎呦，这就是匹诺康尼宣扬的「同谐」吗，真是可怜又可笑啊。
t_en: It was truly wonderful... Ah, is this the Harmony that Penacony touts? It is truly pathetic and laughable.

t_ch: 啧，尽是些没品的家伙…我就不信真找不到一个…嗯？
t_en: Tsk, none of them have any taste... Surely I can find at least one... Hm?

t_ch: 嗨！这位同学，能占用你一点时间吗？我希望能向你介绍一下我们的社团——「怪奇俱乐部」！
t_en: Hi! Fellow student, can I take a moment of your time? I was hoping to introduce you to our school club... the Strange Club!

t_ch: 这社团，它正经吗？
t_en: Is this club official?

t_ch: 那当然！咱可是正规社团，学院里登记在案的，你就放一百个心吧！
t_en: Of course! We're a proper, registered club. Don't you worry about that at all!

t_ch: 没时间，没兴趣。
t_en: No time, not interested.

t_ch: 呃…没兴趣不要紧，你听我讲完就有了！不会耽误太久，真的！
t_en: Uh... If you're not interested now, hear me out and you will be! I won't take much of your time, I promise!

t_ch: 那我就直说了——朋友，你听过「折纸大学七大不可思议」吗？
t_en: I'll get straight to the point... My friend, have you heard of The Seven Wonders of Paperfold University?

t_ch: 学院角落平平无奇的喷泉却是「迷路圣地」，有人说那是幽灵作祟，有人说那是筑梦BUG；知名教授众目睽睽之下化身气球，是人性的扭曲，还是道德的沦丧…等等等等！
t_en: An unremarkable water fountain in a corner of the university is actually a "shrine of the lost." Some say it's haunted by ghosts, others think it is a dreamweaving bug. A famous professor transformed into a balloon in front of a room full of witnesses. Is it a distortion of humanity or the loss of morality...! And that's just the beginning!

t_ch: 怎么样，是不是特刺激，有没有很好奇？先别急，还有更劲爆的呢！
t_en: What do you think? Doesn't it get you fired up? Aren't you super curious? Don't go rushing off yet, I've got even more shocking stuff to come!

t_ch: 「睡蕉小猴」知道吧——那玩意儿在前段时间火得离谱，也消失得突兀。
t_en: You've heard of Slumbernana Monkey, right? That thing was trending like crazy not long ago, but now it's vanished into thin air just as quickly.

t_ch: 原本大家还对小猴爱得死去活来，到处「蕉蕉蕉蕉」呢，就一天不到的功夫，所有人都去讨论《银河忍法帖》了。
t_en: Everyone was obsessed with that little monkey. All you'd hear was "bana bana bana." Then, before a day had even passed, everyone moved on to that Cosmic Ninjutsu Inscriptions.

t_ch: 出于对怪谈的敏锐嗅觉，我开始着手调查，但你猜怎么着？所有曾经的小猴粉丝都表现得——特别正常！
t_en: I have a very keen sense when it comes to ghost stories, so I began researching it. And do you know what I found? All of these people who were monkey fans all seemed... completely normal!

t_ch: 你的调查结果就这？
t_en: That was the conclusion of your research?

t_ch: 咳。别急嘛，你不觉得这才是最奇怪的吗？就算匹诺康尼潮流迭代再怎么快，也不该如此不留痕迹…先叠个甲啊，我没说芮克先生的新作不好，我自己也是他的粉丝。
t_en: Ahem. Hold on now, don't you think that is precisely what makes it strange? Trends in Penacony may change quickly, but it's not normal for them to just vanish without a trace, is it? Although, I should say that I don't mean Mr. Reca's work is no good, I'm a fan of his, too.

t_ch: 总之这很难不让人去怀疑，事情背后是否有什么阴谋。比如一场家族针对学生们秘密进行的精神实验，再比如梦境的结构发生根本改变…
t_en: Either way, I can't help but suspect that there is some kind of secret plot behind all this. I mean, what if this is some kind of secret psychological experiment that The Family carried out on students? Or maybe the structure of the Dreamscape itself suddenly experienced a fundamental structural transformation...

t_ch: 问我，我都知道！
t_en: Ask me, I know!

t_ch: 嘶…好家伙，我这是撞大运了？快快快，给我讲讲！
t_en: Ah... I knew it! Have I hit the jackpot? Quick, quick, tell me everything you know!

t_ch: 说起这个你可就不困了——眼见一个系统时过去，你依旧滔滔不绝舌灿莲花毫无疲态。
t_en: As you start to discuss this topic, all of your exhaustion suddenly fades away... A system hour passes before your very eyes as you continue your tireless speech without any sign of slowing down.

t_ch: 呃…要不今天先到这？你这故事咳、我是说，你提供的讯息非常有趣，我会好好记录下来的！
t_en: Uh... Maybe we should leave it here for today? This story of yours — uhh — I mean this information you have given me is extremely interesting. I will make sure I get it all written down!

t_ch: 你还挺敏锐的。
t_en: You have keen senses.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，那是当然！在这方面我的鼻子可是比哈贝克人还灵敏。
t_en: Heh-heh, of course! When it comes to things like this, my nose is sharper than the Harbecks'!

t_ch: 对了，你要是知道些什么，记得情报共享啊。我请你吃恰丽卡学姐的点心，那个现在可是很难抢到的！
t_en: Oh, and if you find out anything else, then make sure to come and share intel with me. I'll treat you to some of senior Chaletka's homemade snacks. They're not easy to get hold of nowadays!

t_ch: 都说好奇害死猫…
t_en: They say that curiosity killed the cat...

t_ch: 就当你是在夸我了…啧，至少不是什么「颠子」、「怪胎」之类的评价……
t_en: I'll take that as a compliment... Tch, at least it's better than being called a "wacko" or a "freak"...

t_ch: 不过说真的，人要是没了好奇心，和下水道里的鳄鱼又有什么区别？
t_en: But seriously, if humanity loses their curiosity, how are they any different from a crocodile crawling around in the sewers?

t_ch: 总之，我准备对「睡蕉小猴」进行更深入的调查。但当务之急…我需要一个得力助手。
t_en: Either way, I'm going to do further research into Slumbernana Monkey. However, given the urgency of the task, I need a capable assistant...

t_ch: 而你——就有这个潜质！现在递交入团申请，你就是除了本社长之外最资深的成员，有权优先享受社团的一切资源福利！
t_en: And you... You've got what it takes! If you submit your application to join now, you'll be the most senior member after me, the club leader! You will be able to enjoy all the benefits and resources our club offers!

t_ch: 怎么样，心动了吗？机会难得，可千万别错过啊！
t_en: What do you think? It's a great honor, right? This is a rare opportunity, make sure you don't miss out!

t_ch: 你还知道什么怪谈？
t_en: Do you know any other ghost stories?

t_ch: 这你可就问对人了！让我想想…这学期比较出名的怪谈有「死亡使者」、「千面杀手」、「折纸小鸟」以及「神秘信使」，你想听哪个？
t_en: You asked the right person! Let me think... The more well-known ghost stories from this semester were the "Envoy of Death," "Thousand-Faced Killer," "Origami Bird," and "Mysterious Messenger." Which one would you like to hear about?

t_ch: 死亡使者…
t_en: The Envoy of Death...

t_ch: 传说梦境中有散播「死亡」的可怖怪兽，一旦被它掳走，人们在现实中的 躯体就会成为一具空壳。
t_en: Rumor has it that there is a terrifying monster in the Dreamscape that spreads "death." If someone is taken away by it, their bodies in the real world will be left as empty shells.

t_ch: 这个怪谈曾是街头巷尾讨论的对象，但不知为何突然销声匿迹了——简直就像这次的「睡蕉小猴」一样。
t_en: This ghost story was a hot topic on the streets a while ago, but it suddenly went quiet... It's just like the Slumbernana Monkey incident.

t_ch: 对此我倒是有个猜测：或许这根本就是家族自导自演的一场戏，目的是借「怪物」之手，清除对梦境有威胁的家伙们……
t_en: I actually have a speculation about this, though: Perhaps this is all nothing more than a performance put on by The Family. They could just be using the identity of a "monster" to clear out people that pose a threat to the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 千面杀手…
t_en: The Thousand-Faced Killer...

t_ch: 有段时间，在匹诺康尼的街头频频发生恐怖袭击事件——人们好端端走在大马路上，或是被人用大锤一通乱砸、或是被人用领带活活勒醒……
t_en: There was a period of time when there were terrifying attacks happening in the streets all the time... People would be minding their business in the streets and would be either smashed in the head with a huge hammer or strangled with a necktie...

t_ch: 虽然每次凶手的样貌、种族、作案手法各不相同，几起案件在形式上却相当一致：现场总会留下三样东西——凶器，受害者的私人物品，以及用途不明的美梦剧团。
t_en: Even though the murderer looked different, was of a different race and used a different M.O. each time, the cases were consistent in one thing: They would always leave three things at the scene of the crime — the weapon, the victim's personal effects, and a Sweet Dreams Troupe member, purpose unknown.

t_ch: 而奇怪的是…如此恶劣的事件，知情者却寥寥无几，甚至就连家族也对此不闻不问——至少表面如此。
t_en: What's even more strange is that... despite the brutality of these attacks, no one seemed to know about them. Even The Family seemed to be completely oblivious... At least that's how it seemed on the surface.

t_ch: 折纸小鸟…
t_en: The Origami Bird...

t_ch: 我有个大胆的猜想：折纸小鸟其实并非单纯的卡通角色，它们真实地存在于匹诺康尼的街头巷尾，但只有极少数的、被选中的人才能看到它们。
t_en: I have a bold theory: That origami birds are not simply cartoon characters, but they actually exist on the streets of Penacony. It's just that only a very small number of selected people can see them.

t_ch: 按照某个故事里的说法，那些「小鸟」是逝于梦中之人所化的幽灵。它们被困在梦里，无法离开，又太过孤独，才会时不时想办法让人们看到自己……
t_en: According to a story I once heard, these "birds" are actually the spirits of people who passed away in a dream. They are trapped inside, unable to escape. It is their unbearable loneliness that leads them to occasionally allow themselves to be seen by us...

t_ch: 简直就像是书上说的「地缚灵」一样，要是我也能看到它们就好了。
t_en: They're just like the "earth-bound spirits" I have read about in books. If only I could see them, too.

t_ch: 神秘信使…
t_en: The Mysterious Messenger...

t_ch: …这件事嘛，消息的来源我不便提及…大概就是说，一伙虚构史学家绑架了橡木家主——大明星知更鸟的哥哥，星期日。
t_en: ...That one, huh? I cannot actually tell you where I got my intel on this, but... the general story is that a group of History Fictionologists kidnapped the Oak Family Head — Sunday, brother of that famous star Robin.

t_ch: 案发现场就在大剧院，绑匪留下了封不知所云的绑架信，通篇都在说什么乌鸦什么的…可真够离谱的，对吧？
t_en: This all happened at the Grand Theater. The kidnappers left some kind of incomprehensible kidnapping note talking about crows or something... Pretty crazy, right?

t_ch: 但据我所知，前段时间橡木家系好像确实发生了什么不得了的变动…至于为何将此事归为怪谈，姑且算我的私心吧——
t_en: As far as I know, though, the Oak Family did actually undergo some huge changes a while back... As for why I categorize this as a ghost story... let's just say it's my personal interest.

t_ch: 那封信落款的署名，来自于一部有名的怪奇小说，而小说衍生的同人广播电台最近恰巧开播了。
t_en: The name signed on that letter comes from a famous horror novel, the fan radio station for which just so happened to start broadcasting recently.

t_ch: 你忙吧，不打扰了。
t_en: I'll let you get on.

t_ch: 不不不，一点都不打扰，想听怪谈的话，随时都可以来找我！
t_en: No, no! You're not interrupting me at all. If it's ghost stories you're after, you can come and see me anytime!

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. See ya.

t_ch: 拜拜！记得有空来递交入团申请啊！
t_en: Bye! Don't forget to hand in your club application when you get a moment!

t_ch: 绝对不可能是什么排演事故…我敢用这学期的绩点发誓。
t_en: There is no way that this was just a simple rehearsal accident... I'd bet my semester's grades on it!

t_ch: 嗨，同学！是来递交入团申请的吗？我就知道咱俩肯定合得来——
t_en: Hi, fellow classmate! Are you here to hand in your club application? I just knew that we'd get on...

t_ch: 别紧张，还没准备好也没关系，我又有新素材了，正好和你分享一下！
t_en: No need to worry if you haven't got it ready yet. I've got new material to share with you!

t_ch: 前阵子克劳克影城的怪事你知道吧——一场转瞬即逝的筹码雨之后，天空破开了个巨大的口子。
t_en: You've heard about the weird story that went down at Clock Studios Theme Park a while ago, right? A flood of chips started raining down, then a huge hole opened in the sky.

t_ch: 虽然官方发出声明，说是鸢尾花家系在测试什么「拟梦技术」时发生了机械事故，但这种说辞也就能骗骗傻子。
t_en: Although the official statement was that the Iris Family were testing some kind of DreamSim Tech and there was some kind of mechanical malfunction, only simpletons would fall for an excuse like that.

t_ch: 我从一位「当事人」那里听说，其实是有位公司的高管得罪了巡海游侠，被人追杀到匹诺康尼来了。为了自保，这位高管在克劳克影院设下陷阱，想解决掉游侠，但结果嘛……
t_en: I heard from a witness that there was an IPC official who had gotten on the wrong side of the Galaxy Rangers, and they chased him down to Penacony. To protect himself, this official had laid a trap in Clock Studios, thinking he could take down the Ranger, but it turns out...

t_ch: 虽然那人是这么说的，我总觉得事情不会这么简单。你想啊，能轻而易举地斩断天空…这可不是普通游侠能做到的……
t_en: Although that's what they told me, I don't think it's that simple. Think about it. Cutting the sky in half with ease... That's not something a simple Ranger could do, is it...?

t_ch: 前阵子克劳克影城的怪事你知道吧——一场转瞬即逝的筹码雨之后，天空破开了个巨大的口子。
t_en: You've heard about the weird story that went down at Clock Studios Theme Park a while ago, right? A flood of chips started raining down, then a huge hole opened in the sky.

t_ch: 虽然官方发出声明，说是鸢尾花家系在测试什么「拟梦技术」时发生了机械事故，但这种说辞也就能骗骗傻子。
t_en: Although the official statement was that the Iris Family were testing some kind of DreamSim Tech and there was some kind of mechanical malfunction, only simpletons would fall for an excuse like that.

t_ch: 我从一位「当事人」那里听说，其实是有位公司的高管得罪了巡海游侠，被人追杀到匹诺康尼来了。为了自保，这位高管在克劳克影院设下陷阱，想解决掉游侠，但结果嘛……
t_en: I heard from a witness that there was an IPC official who had gotten on the wrong side of the Galaxy Rangers, and they chased him down to Penacony. To protect himself, this official had laid a trap in Clock Studios, thinking he could take down the Ranger, but it turns out...

t_ch: 不过虽然那人是这么说的，我总觉得事情不会这么简单。你想啊，能轻而易举地斩断天空…这可不是普通游侠能做到的……
t_en: Although that's what they told me, I don't think it's that simple. Think about it. Cutting the sky in half with ease... That's not something a simple Ranger could do, is it...?

t_ch: …不行…集中精神，不能…睡……
t_en: ...It's no good... Focus, you mustn't... sleep...

t_ch: …唔…小点声，不要再爬来爬去的了…磁带摩擦的声音太吵…听不清广播了……
t_en: ...Ahh... Quiet, stop crawling around... The sound of the tape is too loud... I can't hear the broadcast clearly...

t_ch: 别、别晃！你到底有多少眼睛啊？再晃我就要吐啦…停一停…停一停……
t_en: Stop, stop shaking! How many eyes do you have!? Stop shaking, or I'm going to hurl... Stop... Please stop...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: …嗯…你是…是你…我怎么了…？
t_en: ...Hm? You're... It's you... What's going on...?

t_ch: 我刚刚…在干什么来着？
t_en: What... was I doing just now?

t_ch: 神色恍惚的丽姬娅向你走来，却左脚踩右脚，一头扎进大地母亲的怀抱。少女霎时眼圈通红，眼神却恢复了清明。
t_en: Ligeia walked towards you in a daze. Her left leg stumbled over the other and sent her crashing into the embrace of Mother Earth below her. The young girl's eyes redden with unshed tears, but she seems to have finally regained her senses.

t_ch: （继续旁观）
t_en: (Keep observing)

t_ch: …嗯…你是…是你吗，修女小姐…你在哪…好痛啊，救救我吧……
t_en: ...Hmm... You're... Is it you? Miss Nun... Where are you... It hurts, save me...

t_ch: …拒绝…没有…深眠…我不想…还不是——嗷！
t_en: ...I refuse... I will not... slumber... I don't want... It's not... Oww!

t_ch: 痛苦挣扎的丽姬娅一头撞上了墙壁，只听「铛——」的一声脆响，少女霎时眼圈通红，眼神却恢复了清明。
t_en: Writhing in pain, Ligeia hit her head against the wall as you heard a crisp "thud." The young girl's eyes redden with unshed tears, but she seems to have finally regained her senses.

t_ch: 嘶——痛痛痛…啧，我刚刚又陷入谵妄状态了，对吧？
t_en: Ow... It hurts, it hurts... Tsk, I was in a trance again, wasn't I?

t_ch: 就说那个电台绝对有问题，他们还不信…啊，抱歉，这件事有些危险，学校里很多人都中招了，所以我不打算邀请你一起调查。
t_en: I knew there was something wrong with that radio station. They still don't believe me... Ah, sorry, this matter is quite dangerous. Many students have already fallen victim. I will not invite you to help me investigate this one.

t_ch: 不过…这个你倒是可以看看——答应我，如果遇到了和「锈墨」电台有关的事情，一定要及时离得远远的，好吗？
t_en: Although... you can keep an eye out for something for me... Promise me, if you come across anything related to Rusty Ink Radio Station, you stay well away, okay?

t_ch: 啧，就说那个电台绝对有问题，他们还不信…啊，抱歉，这件事有些危险，学校里很多人都中招了，所以我不打算邀请你一起调查。
t_en: Tsk, I knew there was something wrong with that radio station. they still don't believe me... Ah, sorry, this matter is quite dangerous. Many students have already fallen victim. I will not invite you to help me investigate this one.

t_ch: 答应我，如果遇到了和「锈墨」电台有关的事情，一定要及时离得远远的，好吗？
t_en: Promise me, if you come across anything related to Rusty Ink Radio Station, you stay well away, okay?

t_ch: 神经病啊！自己不吃辣的，还得所有人陪你一起啃水煮白菜？
t_en: Is something wrong with you!? You think just because you can't handle spicy food that everyone around you would have to live on boiled cabbage?

t_ch: 我就不明白了，有些人到底哪来的优越感——爱好小众怎么了，一没花你苜蓿币，二没喝你苏乐达，管这么宽干嘛？
t_en: I don't know where everyone gets their sense of superiority... What's wrong with having a niche hobby? I'm not spending your Alfalfa credits or drinking your SoulGlad, so why are you sticking your nose into my business?

t_ch: 整天见了人一口一个「颠子」、一口一个「怪胎」…啧，再「哔哔哔」个没完，小心我往你们汽水里塞老鼠！
t_en: All day, I have people calling me "wacko" and "weirdo"... Tsk. If you keep up your whining, I'll stick a rat in your soda!

t_ch: 少女的眼睛闪闪发亮，让人不由想起学校中央永不熄灭的灯光。
t_en: The young girl's eyes sparkle with a light reminiscent of the undying flames of the campus lamps.

t_ch: 怎么样，我的好助手，准备好和我一起探索「折纸大学七大不可思议」中的第八个——「睡蕉小猴」了吗？
t_en: What do you think, my trusty assistant? Are you ready to explore the eighth of The Seven Wonders of Paperfold University — the Slumbernana Monkey?

t_ch: 七大不可思议…
t_en: The Seven Wonders...

t_ch: 为什么有八个？
t_en: Why are there eight now?

t_ch: 谁说只有八个了？除去小猴，还有个新收录的怪谈叫「锈墨电台」…喂，你那是什么表情？四大天王有五个，七大不可思议有九个，这不是常识吗？
t_en: There are not just eight, you know! It's not just the monkey, there's even the newly recorded ghost story of the Rusty Ink Radio Station, too! ...Hey, what's with that look on your face? There are five people in the Formidable Four, right? It's only normal that there would be nine of the Seven Wonders, right?

t_ch: 看来有时间得给你好好补补课…放心吧，我可是专门整理过每个怪谈的详细内容，给你当教材足够了！
t_en: Looks like I'll have to fill you in when we get time... Fear not, I have organized all of the details on every ghost story. You'll have plenty of materials to learn from!

t_ch: 四大天王有五个，七大不可思议有九个，这不是常识吗？
t_en: There are five people in the Formidable Four, right? It's only normal that there would be nine of the Seven Wonders, right?

t_ch: 看来有时间得给你好好补补课…放心吧，我可是专门整理过每个怪谈的详细内容，给你当教材足够了！
t_en: Looks like I'll have to fill you in when we get time... Fear not, I have organized all of the details on every ghost story. You'll have plenty of materials to learn from!

t_ch: 啊，你好，是新来的社员吗？我好像没见过你...噢，看你这打扮，我想起来了——
t_en: Ah, hello! Are you the new member? I don't think I've met you before... Oh, I recognize that outfit...

t_ch: 你就是传说中的那位无名客啊，没想到能在这里遇见你，真是我的荣幸。
t_en: You are the legendary Nameless, aren't you? I never imagined I would meet you here. It is a true honor!

t_ch: 对了，我还没自我介绍呢。我叫诺伯蒂，目前是刚加入喜剧社的成员，主要是写写剧本、编排节目、做点幕后工作。
t_en: Oh, I haven't introduced myself. My name is Nobody. I've just recently joined the comedy club. I mainly write scripts, edit programs, and do behind-the-scenes work.

t_ch: 有时候社里缺人手，我也会客串一些龙套角色，偶尔在梦泡里露个脸。运气好的话，你说不定能看到我的正脸特写镜头呢。
t_en: Sometimes we're short on people, so I'll do walk-on cameos or show my face in a dream bubble. If you're really lucky, you might get to see a close-up of me.

t_ch: 喜剧社是做什么的？
t_en: What does the comedy club do?

t_ch: 顾名思义，主要是搞即兴表演和小品创作的社团，除了现场演出，我们还会不定期地制作一些梦泡，把表演录好去外面售卖，给大家一个沉浸式体验喜剧的机会。
t_en: As the name suggests, we're a club that mainly puts on improv shows and creates skits. As well as live performances, we also occasionally produce dream bubbles, recording our performances to sell externally and giving people a chance to experience an immersive comedy.

t_ch: 听上去就是个跑龙套的…
t_en: You just sound like an extra?

t_ch: 嗯，我就是大家口中「那个跑龙套的」，但我觉得无所谓呀，凡事总要有第一步的，慢慢来嘛。
t_en: Yeah, I'm the definition of a "walk-on extra." I don't mind though. Everyone has to start somewhere. What's the hurry?

t_ch: 只要能做自己喜欢的事情，能把喜剧当成一门艺术来钻研，对我来说就很满足了。至于名气之类的，这些就随缘吧。
t_en: Just being able to do what I love and study comedy as an art is extremely satisfying for me. As for fame and fortune, that's all down to fate.

t_ch: 说来惭愧，其实我加入社团之后也写过什么像样的段子，没办法，学业压力大，我只能抽空来参加社团活动。
t_en: I'm ashamed to say it, but I haven't really written anything decent since joining the club. With all the pressure from my studies, I can only make time to attend club events when I can.

t_ch: 而且，比起自己登台演出，我还是更喜欢看前辈们的表演…没办法，他们实在太有魅力了，一举一动都是节目效果。
t_en: Plus, I prefer to watch my seniors perform than to take to the stage myself... I can't help it. They're too talented. Their every move just screams entertainment.

t_ch: 虽然因为植入广告的事，在折纸论坛上被骂得有点惨，但那只是梦泡经过夸张处理后的效果，现场表演不是那么回事。
t_en: Although we got massively chewed out for product placement on the Paperfold Forum, it was just because of the exaggerated effects added when our performances were converted to dream bubbles. The live performances were never that bad.

t_ch: 哎，真是搞不懂，就事论事有那么难嘛？有机会的话，你还是来现场看看吧，观感绝对和梦泡见到的不一样。
t_en: Ah, I just don't get it. Is it really that hard to just appreciate our performances for what they are? If they get the opportunity, they should just come and watch us perform live. The experience is always going to be different from watching us in a dream bubble.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 说吧，我可喜欢和你这样有见识的人交流了。
t_en: Say it. I love conversing with cultured people like yourself.

t_ch: 给我介绍下你们社团呗？
t_en: Tell me more about your club?

t_ch: 我们喜剧社成立也有点年头了，本来只是小众社团，制作梦泡的初衷也只是想留个纪念，给社团挣点外快维持运转用的。
t_en: Our comedy club has been around for a few years now. We were small at first. Our original inspiration for making dream bubbles was just to leave some kind of memento and help earn some extra cash to maintain operations.

t_ch: 谁知最近走了大运，有几个前辈的脱口秀反响很好，梦泡都卖光了。连匹诺康尼电视台都闻声赶来，想跟我们签优先购买协议。
t_en: Unexpectedly, we had a recent stroke of luck with a few of our seniors received a great reception on their stand-up shows, and our dream bubbles sold out. Even the Penacony TV Station came running to us hoping to sign a priority purchase agreement.

t_ch: 前辈们看现在人气不错，就接了些广告，却引起了不少争议。不说折纸论坛吧，光是我们喜剧社内部也有不少反对的声音，担心过度商业化会影响创作。
t_en: These seniors realized that they were getting popular and accepted some advertising deals, which actually caused quite a lot of controversy. It wasn't just the Paperfold Forum — Even many of our own club members were growing concerned that over-commercialization could affect our works.

t_ch: 其实大家的顾虑我能理解，但我也不好对前辈们的决策说三道四。
t_en: Honestly, I can understand their concerns. But I have no right to criticize the decisions of my seniors.

t_ch: 你平时都在忙些什么？
t_en: What do you usually do?

t_ch: 我还是学生，平时当然是以学业为主啦。筑梦学院的作业多，已经够忙了。好不容易挤出点课余时间，我就创作点剧本，编排下节目什么的。
t_en: I'm still a student, so naturally I'm normally busy with my studies. I'm already very busy with the mountains of homework on the Dreamweaver Academy's end. When I finally manage to secure some free time, I create scripts and edit shows and stuff.

t_ch: 偶尔在喜剧社里客串演出，帮前辈们录制下梦泡什么的。
t_en: Sometimes I make cameo appearances or help my seniors record dream bubbles.

t_ch: 忙是真忙，累也是真累。不过能做自己喜欢的事情，再苦再累我也乐意。
t_en: I'm always ridiculously busy and tired. That said, if it means I can do what I'm passionate about, I don't mind the tiredness.

t_ch: 有什么社团八卦听吗？
t_en: Is there any gossip in the club?

t_ch: 还能有什么八卦？之前发售的梦泡里加了些植入广告，在折纸论坛上出现了不少负面评价，结果引来老观众们疯狂吐槽。
t_en: What kind of gossips are you expecting? We recently put some product placement into our dream bubbles, which attracted a lot of negative criticism on the Paperfold Forum and a crazy amount of complaints from viewers.

t_ch: 虽说初衷是为社团创收，筹备点翻修舞台的资金。但这些贴子在我们社团内传疯了，现在内部争议挺大的——究竟该不该商业化发展，大家都说不准。
t_en: Although these were originally intended to generate income for the club and raise funds to repair our stage, these posts have been shared like crazy throughout our club. There's a lot of internal conflict now about whether we should allow ourselves to commercialize and develop. No one is quite sure.

t_ch: 还有人眼红那几个走红的前辈，冷嘲热讽地说了几句，前辈们不服气，反击回去。这一来一回，互相数落对方的，又扯出了不少陈年往事。
t_en: Some people were also jealous of our popular seniors and made some sassy retorts about them. Our seniors wouldn't stand for this and clapped back. A lot of shade was thrown back and forth, and a lot of old memories were dragged up.

t_ch: 我反正懒得看他们那点事，喜剧是要带给人快乐的，这种笑不出来的事情，我反正不想搭理。
t_en: I don't have the energy to get involved with these trivial matters, though. Comedy is supposed to make people happy! I don't want to pay any mind to things like this that won't make anyone laugh.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好的，那回头再聊呗。
t_en: Alright, let's chat again soon.

t_ch: 不知道你有没有看过我们喜剧社的梦泡，有条件的话，我还是建议你来现场看，和梦泡里完全是两回事。
t_en: I don't know if you've seen any of our club's dream bubbles? If you can, I would recommend that you come and see us live — it's a completely different experience.

t_ch: 至于那些梦泡的评价，我不建议你去看。不管是好评还是差评，我觉得都不够客观…但没办法，哈，屁股决定脑袋…人生来如此。
t_en: As for comments on the dream bubbles, I would advise against reading them. Be it good comments or bad ones, I don't think they're objective enough... Can't be helped though. Your perspective defines your logic... That's just how life is.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 那我们回头再聊吧。
t_en: Then let's chat again soon.

t_ch: 不知道你有没有看过我们喜剧社的梦泡，有条件的话，我还是建议你来现场看，和梦泡里完全是两回事。
t_en: I don't know if you've seen any of our club's dream bubbles? If you can, I would recommend that you come and see us live — it's a completely different experience.

t_ch: 至于那些梦泡的评价，我不建议你去看。不管是好评还是差评，我觉得都不够客观…但没办法，哈，屁股决定脑袋…人生来如此。
t_en: As for comments on the dream bubbles, I would advise against reading them. Be it good comments or bad ones, I don't think they're objective enough... Can't be helped though. Your perspective defines your logic... That's just how life is.

t_ch: 我看看是怎么个事儿？
t_en: What is this about?

t_ch: …啧，群聊开始了，看来是又吵起来了，跟你说会话的功夫这就弹了一大堆消息出来。
t_en: ...Tsk, it's the group chat. Looks like people are arguing again. Look at all these messages that have piled up just while I was talking with you.

t_ch: 我看看是谁发的…哦，刚加入的新生……
t_en: Let's see who's been sending them... Oh, it's a new student...

t_ch: 哈，屁股决定脑袋，自己还要靠家里养呢，当然不会理解喜剧人的吃饭问题了。
t_en: Ah. Your perspective defines your logic. What would a kid that still lives off their parents know about the struggles a comedian faces to feed themselves?

t_ch: 要我说，追求独立创作也好，迎合商业潮流也罢，说到底，不过是在生存和理想间做出选择罢了。谁能说自己的选择就一定是对的呢？
t_en: If you ask me, it doesn't matter if you're making independent works or going with the commercial trends. At the end of the day, you're doing nothing more than making a decision between survival and your ideals. Who can say that one choice is ultimately more correct?

t_ch: 人各有志，强求不来。与其对着不可知的将来瞎想，倒不如脚踏实地，把基本功练好。
t_en: Everyone has their own ideals. They cannot be forced. I think that, rather than focusing on an unknown future, it is best to go one step at a time and master your fundamental skills.

t_ch: 说到底，喜剧表演还是得扎根在舞台上，有空吵架，还不如多琢磨点段子呢。
t_en: At the end of the day, comedy lives and dies on the stage. If you have time to argue, why not spend it honing your jokes instead?

t_ch: 理想和现实只能二选一吗……
t_en: Are ideals and reality mutually exclusive...?

t_ch: 理想，到底算是前进的动力，还是不切实际的幻梦？说实话，我也没想明白。
t_en: Ideals... Are they a driving force to move one forward, or just unrealistic fantasy? If I'm honest, I haven't figured it out myself either.

t_ch: 还记得当初满怀热忱地加入喜剧社时，我以为自己找到了人生的归宿。总想着凭一己之力为匹诺康尼带来更多欢声笑语。
t_en: I remember that, when I first joined the comedy club, my heart was filled with passion. I thought I had found my destiny. I wanted to use my skills to bring more laughter to Penacony.

t_ch: 后来我才发现，生活根本没有我想的那么浪漫。我在筑梦学院念书，为了生计，学的也是筑梦师的本事。
t_en: Only later did I realize that life was not so romantic. I study at Dreamweaver Academy, and I major in Dreamweaving just so I can hope to make a living.

t_ch: 工程作业已经够多了，还要出去搬砖…从基层做起，这就是筑梦师的生活。每天光是活着就已经很累了，根本没时间去构思脱口秀的内容编排……
t_en: I'm already buried in engineering homework, then I have to go and do physical construction tasks... Working your way up from the lowest builder is the life of a dreamweaver. Just living in that way is already exhausting. How am I supposed to have time to think up content and jokes for talk shows after all that...

t_ch: 哎，有时候我在想，我是不是该放弃自己的理想…比起一夜成名这种梦，果然还是老实打灰，考到筑梦师资格证更现实吧。
t_en: Ah... Sometimes I wonder if I should just give up on my ideals... Maybe making an honest living in engineering and getting my dreamweaver qualification is more realistic than some dream of overnight fame.

t_ch: 人活着总得有点爱好…
t_en: People always need a hobby...

t_ch: 怎么能放弃自己的梦想呢？
t_en: How can you give up on your dream like that?

t_ch: 老实说，我想过这个问题，我的未来一眼就能望到头——没什么天赋，注定只能跑跑龙套，当个业余段子手。这种水平完全不足以养活自己……
t_en: Truth be told, I've thought about this question before. My future is plain as day — mediocre in talent, destined to be an extra on the stage, working freelance as a joke writer. I won't be able to eke a living with my meager skills...

t_ch: 但我想要试试看，不然就这么放弃，我还是不甘心…何况我们喜剧社已经实现自负盈亏了，这给了我信心。
t_en: But I want to take a shot at it. I'm not ready to give up yet, especially since our comedy club is now self-sustaining. That gives me hope.

t_ch: 说不定下一个爆红的就是我…未来的事情谁说得准呢。
t_en: Who knows... I might become the next big star... Who can tell with the future?

t_ch: 理想和现实，就像平行线，永远都不会有交集。
t_en: Dreams and reality are like parallel lines that will never intersect.

t_ch: 曾几何时，我以为有梦想就能活下去。但我也快毕业了，生存问题还是更要紧些……
t_en: There was a time when I thought I could survive with dreams alone. But I'm graduating soon — survival is probably a more urgent priority right now...

t_ch: 不过…时代在变，环境在变，我们喜剧社也不一样了。靠销售梦泡基本能达成收支平衡，还有植入广告之类的花样手段来赚钱，比过去的状况好多了。
t_en: But... times have changed, so has our environment, and so has the comedy club. To be able to sell dream bubbles to make ends meet and earn some cash with product placements and other similar tactics... We're much better than in the past.

t_ch: 就是广告带来的恶评不太好…哎，这点我倒是能接受……
t_en: The poor reviews from the advertisements aren't great... *sigh* But I can accept that...

t_ch: 商业化？原来只是个笑话！
t_en: Commercialization? So it's just a joke!

t_ch: 哼，说什么商业化发展，我觉得只是社团走投无路的借口！
t_en: Hmph, what's this talk about commercialization and development? I think it's just an excuse from a club that has run out of options!

t_ch: 真是讽刺，曾经高喊着要用喜剧改变世界的人，转眼就把立场和初心统统抛诸脑后。说出来的大道理，翻脸就可以当笑话。
t_en: How ironic. Those who once yelled about how they would change the world with comedy have quickly cast aside their principles and ideals. The lofty ideals they once spoke of are nothing more than a joke now.

t_ch: 可笑，他们口口声声说梦想，到头来，不还是向生存低了头？一旦触及利益，什么理想不理想的，哪里有植入广告带来的收成香！
t_en: What a joke. They constantly speak of dreams, yet in the end, they still bow in order to survive. Once profit is involved, who cares about dreams and ideals? The smell of money from ads is what matters most!

t_ch: 没错！我也骂我自己！要是我再努点力，每天多想几个好玩的段子，是不是社团的人气就能更高些，就不用去接这些无聊的广告了？
t_en: That's right! I'm scolding myself, too! If only I worked harder and came up with a few more jokes, would the club have been more popular? Maybe then we wouldn't have to take on these boring ads.

t_ch: 我…真是没用啊！
t_en: I'm... so useless!

t_ch: 其实这样也挺好！
t_en: Actually, this is pretty good!

t_ch: 其实我觉得我们社团走商业化路线也挺好啊！
t_en: Actually, I think it's pretty good that our club is moving into a commercialized route!

t_ch: 以前从场地到演出，所有环节都要我们自己掏钱，光是租金就要一大笔钱，光靠卖门票的那点收入哪里够呀！
t_en: Back then, from the venue to the performance, everything was paid for out of our own pockets. Just the rent alone took up a huge chunk, and the ticket sales were nowhere enough to cover it!

t_ch: 没想到前辈们的思路竟如此清奇，老实说我也是才发现，原来广告还有这么多花式植入的方法…要我说，只要段子质量高，广告多点也无妨嘛。
t_en: I didn't expect our seniors to have such an out-of-the-box idea. Honestly, I've only just realized how many creative ways there are to integrate ads... If the jokes are good, I don't mind having more ads.

t_ch: 而且观众们骂归骂，前辈们那几期演出还不是爆火了？电视台还上门拉我们签合同呢，这样下去，说不定哪天…我们社团在匹诺康尼电视台就拥有专属节目了！
t_en: Even with all the complaints from the audience, the previous few shows from the seniors still went viral! The TV station even came to us with a contract! If this continues, who knows... maybe our club will have our own show on the Penacony TV Station one day!

t_ch: 到那个时候…说不定我也能在电视上常驻露面，天啊…我都不敢想了！
t_en: When that time comes... who knows? I might be a regular on TV! Oh gee... I don't even dare to imagine that!

t_ch: …音乐派对的演出结束了，乐器搬走了，乐队成员都散了，我的心也变得空荡荡的了。
t_en: The music party's performance has ended, the instruments have been moved away, and the band members have all gone their separate ways. My heart is empty now.

t_ch: 将我的美好回忆，弃置于此——
t_en: I'll leave my precious memories here.

t_ch: 其余成员不都还在嘛。
t_en: But the other members are still here.

t_ch: 乱破小姐走了啊！她才是「缭乱」的灵魂，虽然现在还会和其他成员碰面，但聊起天来，总感觉少了点什么。
t_en: Miss Rappa left! She's the soul of The Dazzling! We can still meet up with the other members, but it still feels like something is missing.

t_ch: 对演出的热情？那种胡闹的气氛？还是不管学院阻拦，坚持要冲上舞台的勇气？
t_en: The passion for performing? The rowdy atmosphere? Or the courage to push ahead to perform despite the disapproval from the schools?

t_ch: 哎，我也说不清楚。只是…演出结束后，再去那些派对都觉得没劲了。
t_en: *sigh* I can't really put it into words. It's just that... after the performance, going to all those parties feels dull.

t_ch: 你不是要去学忍术吗？
t_en: I thought you were gonna go learn some ninjutsu?

t_ch: 学忍术？呃…我当时是这么说的嘛，好像有点记不清了。让我想想，想想……
t_en: Learn ninjutsu? Hmm... Did I say that? I don't quite remember. Let me think. Let me think...

t_ch: 财富学院都这么善变的吗？
t_en: Are Fortune Academy students all so capricious?

t_ch: 你又在骗我了！
t_en: You're lying to me again!

t_ch: 哎呀，计划总是赶不上变化，我的兴趣爱好那么丰富，换个人生目标也很正常。
t_en: Oh well, plans always change, and I have so many interests and hobbies. It's normal to switch to a different life goal.

t_ch: 对！我想起来了，让乱破小姐教我忍术！实不相瞒，演出结束后，我确实跟着乱破小姐学了些基本技巧，可那实在太难了，很折磨。
t_en: Yes! I remember now, I wanted to ask Miss Rappa to teach me ninjutsu! To tell you the truth, after the show ended, I learned plenty of fundamentals from Miss Rappa, even though they were excruciatingly hard.

t_ch: 什么忍符的绘制技巧，忍•真言的吟诵韵律，超•忍•动态视力的磨练…比我在健身房上的私教课还要累，我是想做忍侠，不是来受罪的呀。
t_en: What's all this nonsense about ninja talisman drawing techniques, ninja mantra chant cadence, super ninja dynamic vision training... They're even more tiring than my private gym lessons! I want to become a Ninja Hero! Not to feel like dying all the time!

t_ch: 所以我就放弃啦。世界上的各种*侠*那么多，也不缺我一个吧。
t_en: That's why I gave up. There are all kinds of *heroes* all around the world. It won't make a difference without me.

t_ch: 那你后续有什么打算？
t_en: So what's next for you?

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你问了个好问题。我最近也在烦恼这件事。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager, you've asked a great question. I've been troubled over this recently too.

t_ch: 就快毕业了，但我还没想好离开学校后该去做什么，是找份正经的工作，还是留在学院里继续深造，或者…做个独立音乐人也不错？
t_en: It's almost time for graduation, but I haven't thought of what to do next. Do I find a decent job, stay in school for further education, or... become an independent musician?

t_ch: 我们共同经历了那么多事，彼此都很熟悉啦。在经理看来，最适合我的出路是什么呢？
t_en: We've gone through so much together. We know each other well. In your eyes, what do you think I'm most suitable for?

t_ch: 去上班吧！
t_en: Go to work!

t_ch: 我也想去职场闯荡，可…经理你也知道，现在的工作不太好找，轻松赚钱的职位更是少之又少，我又不想离开匹诺康尼…很麻烦啊。
t_en: I'd also like to venture into the workforce, but... as you know, it's difficult to land a job now, much less get a relaxing job with easy money. And I don't want to leave Penacony... It's a hassle.

t_ch: 继续深造会比较好。
t_en: It's better to further your studies.

t_ch: 我的导师也这么劝我，还说我不做他的学生实在可惜了，可我怎么觉得…他是希望我留在学校帮他干活呢？可以随便使唤，还不用付钱。
t_en: My teacher gave me the same advice and even said it would be a waste if I didn't become his student. But somehow I feel... he just hopes that I stay in school so I can help him with his stuff. He can order me around without even paying me a wage.

t_ch: 不行不行，我对学术本来就没多少兴趣，再待下去未免太浪费人生了。
t_en: No can do. I've never had much interest in academia. I would be wasting my life away if I stayed on.

t_ch: 我觉得你很有音乐天赋。
t_en: I think you do have musical talent.

t_ch: …是吗？我也这么觉得！从站上舞台的时候开始，我就想把余生都奉献给音乐了！
t_en: ...Really? I think so too! From the moment I stood on stage, I knew I wanted to give my life to music!

t_ch: 但…把兴趣当职业，是不是有点太理想了？要是火不起来怎么办，赚不到钱，甚至连票都卖不出去又该怎么办？光是想到这些，我就开始害怕了。
t_en: But... to turn my interest into a job... is it too idealistic? What if I don't become popular? What if I can't earn any money or even sell any tickets? Just thinking about this makes me afraid.

t_ch: 要不，还是老老实实找份工作算了吧？
t_en: Then, should I just forget about it and look for a job?

t_ch: …你还是自己决定吧。
t_en: ...You best decide for yourself.

t_ch: 太难了。我想不出来。
t_en: It's too difficult. I can't think of an answer.

t_ch: 哎，算了算了，也别烦恼了。我还是尽快拿到毕业证书，回去继承家里的企业好了。
t_en: *sigh* Forget it. I shall not worry anymore. I better quickly get my graduation certificate and return home to take over the family business.

t_ch: 既然找不到合适的方向，说明每条路都不适合我。有简单的模式可以选，我也别给自己的人生增加难度了。
t_en: Since I can't find the right direction, that means that none of the paths are suitable for me. If l can choose an easy mode for life, I shouldn't make it any harder than it needs to be.

t_ch: 那你还问我？
t_en: Then why did you even ask me?

t_ch: 你这不是没有任何烦恼吗！
t_en: Doesn't this mean you never had any troubles to begin with?

t_ch: 面临人生的关键抉择，总还是会迷茫。再说，继承家业需要考虑的事情也很多呀。
t_en: Facing important life decisions can be quite overwhelming. After all, taking over the family business does involve a lot of things too.

t_ch: 谢谢你帮我做职业分析，经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}！
t_en: Thank you for helping me with my career analysis, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager!

t_ch: …音乐派对的演出结束了，乐器搬走了，乐队成员都散了，我的心也变得空荡荡的了。
t_en: The music party's performance has ended, the instruments have been moved away, and the band members have all gone their separate ways. My heart is empty now.

t_ch: 将我的美好回忆，弃置于此——
t_en: I'll leave my precious memories here.

t_ch: 你有事情要找我吗？
t_en: Do you need me for something?

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你来得正好。你知道吗，学校要出毕业年鉴了，每个学院的学生都有自己的专属页面。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager, your timing is great. Did you know the school will be releasing its yearbook soon? Every student will have their own dedicated page.

t_ch: 我想请你帮我写段留言…毕竟我们一起举办过那么精彩的演出，经历过那么多重要的时刻，不知道你现在方不方便？
t_en: I was hoping to get you to write a message for me... After all, we've organized many exciting performances together and experienced many crucial moments. Would you be available now?

t_ch: （写下严肃的寄语）
t_en: (Write a serious message)

t_ch: （写下幽默的寄语）
t_en: (Write a witty message)

t_ch: （写下煽情的寄语）
t_en: (Write an emotional message)

t_ch: 让我看看…太好了，真适合我，不愧是经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}！短短几句话就令我的页面与众不同！
t_en: Let me see... That's amazing, it suits me so well! No wonder you're our {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager! You've made my page stand out with just a few sentences!

t_ch: 对了，我也给你复制一份，这样就不用等年鉴正式刊发出来了。千万别忘了我啊！
t_en: Oh yeah, I've made you a copy, so you don't have to wait for the yearbook to be published. Don't forget me!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}太客气了！我们都这么熟悉了，有什么事就直说吧！
t_en: You're too polite, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager! We are so familiar with each other, just speak freely!

t_ch: 你还会和乐队成员联系吗？
t_en: Will you still keep in contact with the other band members?

t_ch: 会啊。我们是并肩作战的同伴，彼此间的信任早就坚不可摧了，这种深厚的友谊，可不是派对上认识的那些损友能比的！
t_en: Of course. We are comrades that stood by each other through thick and thin. Our trust in each other is unshakable. This deep-rooted friendship can't be compared to those fair-weather friends you meet at parties!

t_ch: 我们会定期碰面，聊聊学校的话题，各自的近况，还有未来的规划…哎，不过乱破小姐走了，见面时候总感觉少了点什么。
t_en: We'll meet regularly and chat about school, update each other on what's going on recently, and discuss future plans... *sigh* But with Miss Rappa's departure, it feels like something is missing whenever we meet.

t_ch: …「缭乱」不会解散，但她才是我们乐队的核心啊。
t_en: ...The Dazzling will not disband, but she is the lead of our band.

t_ch: 财富学院还有为难你吗？
t_en: Is Fortune Academy still giving you a hard time?

t_ch: 哈哈，说到这个，自从「缭乱」把那个肆意妄为的学院代表赶走后，我在学院里的地位可谓是如日中天啊，同学和老师们都对我很尊敬。
t_en: Haha, speaking of this, ever since The Dazzling chased away that unscrupulous academy representative, my standing in the academy has soared. All my classmates and teachers greatly respect me now.

t_ch: 也是他咎由自取吧，原本就有很多学生看不惯他，如今总算能出这口恶气了。
t_en: He brought it on himself. Many students already disliked him, so now they finally can get back at him.

t_ch: 现在，我是财富学院的英雄——忍者•契波大人！哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Now, I'm the hero of Fortune Academy — Master Ninja Czipo! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}想聊天的时候，随时来找我就行！
t_en: If you ever want to chat, do look for me anytime!

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …这是什么新流行的搭讪方式？我怎么没在派对上听人说过。
t_en: ...Is this some new pick-up tactic? Why didn't I hear about this at a party?

t_ch: 要是经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}感兴趣的话…当然可以！我们的关系都这么好了，表示下亲昵也很正常。来，试试吧，这正是进一步加深彼此间信任的机会！
t_en: If you're interested... Of course you can! We're so familiar with each other, it's normal to show some affection. Come, try it, this is a good opportunity to deepen our trust!

t_ch: 犹豫片刻后，你将手指轻轻放在了白色绒球上。那柔软的触感使你确信，刚才问出这个失礼问题时的窘迫是值得的，你很愿意为这种体验再次冒险。
t_en: After a moment's hesitation, you lightly place your finger on the white fluff ball. From that soft touch, you are certain that the awkwardness from that rude question just now was worth it. You are very willing to risk your skin for that experience again.

t_ch: 兄弟，你摸起来好舒服。
t_en: Bro, your fluff ball feels nice.

t_ch: 你真好。
t_en: You're really nice.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，能让经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}开心，是我的荣幸！
t_en: Heh-heh, it's an honor to make {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager this happy!

t_ch: 好好享受这场音乐派对吧！
t_en: Enjoy this music party to the fullest!

t_ch: 当然！我得珍惜毕业前最后的欢乐时光才行——派对万岁！音乐万岁！
t_en: I will! I must treasure the final moments of joy before graduation — viva la music party! Viva la music!

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏碰触契波的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，听到的都是相同的话语……
t_en: You attempt to use clockwork to get in touch with Czipo's heart, but no matter how much the clock turns, you hear the same old words...

t_ch: 「——我等乃是『缭乱』！！！」
t_en: "...We are The Dazzling!!!"

t_ch: 看来先前的演出经历在乐队成员心中留下了极为深刻的印象，以至于任何情绪都无法动摇这份热情。这就是音乐的力量吧。
t_en: Looks like the performances left an indelible, lasting impression in the band's hearts — no amount of emotion can cause this passion to waver. This is the power of music.

t_ch: 经理好。音乐派对还没有结束，外面这么热闹，您该去享受欢快的校园生活才对。
t_en: Hello Manager. The music party isn't over yet. It's bustling out there, and you should be enjoying the carefree school life outside.

t_ch: 这里是我们曾经的排练室，不过现在…已经和乐队没什么关系了。
t_en: This was once our rehearsal room, but now... it's got nothing to do with the band.

t_ch: 你为什么要待在这里呢？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: …我吗？我本来就不喜欢吵闹的地方，这里比较僻静，让我觉得安心…也很怀念。
t_en: ...Me? I don't like noisy places. It's much quieter here. It gives me peace... and nostalgia.

t_ch: 跟着乱破忍首修行的那段时间，就像是画面定格了那样，始终在记忆里循环…我和乐队同伴们站在舞台上，向所有人宣告我们的名号。
t_en: While I was training with Head Ninja Rappa, it was like time stood still, and the memories of that time still swirl in my head... Standing on the stage with me and my bandmates, declaring our name to everyone.

t_ch: 对我来说，这样的经历简直难以想象。我大概…永远都忘不掉「缭乱」了吧。
t_en: Such an experience was simply unimaginable to me. I probably... will never forget The Dazzling.

t_ch: 你们当时演奏的乐器呢？
t_en: Where are the instruments you guys used for the show?

t_ch: 演出结束后，乱破忍首曾经拜托工作人员将乐器都搬到这里，但我和艾尔菲同学把各自的乐器带回了家…重要的物品，放在教室里不太放心。
t_en: After the show ended, Head Ninja Rappa asked a staff member to move the instruments here, but Arfey and I brought ours home... It's not safe to leave important stuff in the classroom.

t_ch: 是向学校申请过的。再说，乐器上绘满了忍符，除了我们应该也没人会使用了。
t_en: We requested them from the school. Also, the instruments have been scribbled full of ninja talismans — no one else but us would use them.

t_ch: 这个键盘…
t_en: This keyboard...

t_ch: 契波同学说是要在校园里留下「缭乱」的印记，就把键盘存放在这里了。
t_en: Czipo said he wanted to leave The Dazzling's mark at the school, so he left the keyboard here.

t_ch: 能在学校里看到忍符…我还挺开心的。
t_en: To be able to see a ninja talisman at school... It makes me pretty happy.

t_ch: 那个DJ台…
t_en: That DJ turntable...

t_ch: 音乐派对还会继续，因此乱破忍首使用的演出设备就留在了舞台上。
t_en: The music party will continue, so Head Ninja Rappa left the performance gear on the stage.

t_ch: 蒙塔娜同学负责管理，我们没事的时候也会去看看。
t_en: Montana's in charge of that. We can go take a look when we're free.

t_ch: 那你后续有什么打算？
t_en: So what's next for you?

t_ch: 我…我还是先完成答辩论文吧，要是没法毕业就麻烦了。
t_en: I... I'd better finish my dissertation first. It'll be troublesome if I can't graduate.

t_ch: 顺利的话，离开学校后，我想组建一只新的乐队——以我为核心的乐队。
t_en: If everything goes well, after I graduate, I plan to form a new band — one where I'm the lead.

t_ch: 乱破忍首向学院代表保证过，会接纳我为演出乐队的正式成员，但我更想从头开始，从召集成员、创作乐曲、打造属于自己的风格开始。
t_en: Head Ninja Rappa promised the academy representatives that I would be included as an official member of the performing band, but I'd rather start all over — recruitment, composition, and creating my own unique style.

t_ch: 我的水平是不太够，但技术可以提升，最重要的…是迈出第一步的勇气。
t_en: I may lack skill, but technique can be honed. Most importantly... it's the courage to take that first step.

t_ch: 要找我继续当乐队经理吗？
t_en: You want me to keep being the band manager?

t_ch: 您有兴趣的话，当然可以！有经理在，我们都不用为作曲的事发愁了。
t_en: If you're interested, absolutely! We won't have to fret over composing tracks with a manager around.

t_ch: 开玩笑的…经理很会照顾成员，也敢为乐队的演出和学院正面交涉，要是您愿意帮忙，我肯定会轻松很多的。
t_en: I'm kidding... You've taken great care of us, and were willing to confront the school about the band's performance. If you agreed to help out, it'd definitely be much easier for me.

t_ch: 会找以前的乐队成员吗？
t_en: Will you find the former band members too?

t_ch: 我问过他们的想法，但可惜…契波同学对组乐队没有兴趣，而艾尔菲同学则是有很明确的目标，打算离开匹诺康尼，就只好作罢了。
t_en: I asked them about it, but... Czipo isn't interested in forming a band, and Arfey's got a clear goal in her mind — she intends to leave Penacony, so I had to abandon that idea.

t_ch: 再说…我也想过，他们的水平都很高，自己是否真的能承担起乱破忍首的职责，帮他们实现各自的忍道呢？我…没这个信心。
t_en: Moreover... I've also thought about it — they are very good. Can I really pick up the mantle of Head Ninja Rappa's role and help them fulfill their own way of the ninja? I... don't have that kind of confidence.

t_ch: 先预祝你成功吧！
t_en: Let me wish you the best of luck in the meantime!

t_ch: 谢谢，但还是请您先预祝我顺利毕业吧。
t_en: Thank you, but I think you should wish for me to successfully graduate first.

t_ch: 经理好。音乐派对还没有结束， 外面这么热闹，您该去享受欢快的校园生活才对。
t_en: Hello manager. The music party isn't over yet. It's bustling out there, and you should be enjoying the carefree school life outside.

t_ch: 这里是我们曾经的排练室，不过现在…已经和乐队没什么关系了。
t_en: This was once our rehearsal room, but now... it's got nothing to do with the band.

t_ch: 你有事情要找我吗？
t_en: Do you need me for something?

t_ch: 经理，我想请你帮个忙，就是…学校要出年鉴了，可以邀请同学给自己写赠言，那个…我在学校里和其他人不太熟，您能帮我写一段吗？
t_en: Manager, I'd like to ask for a favor. Um... the school's yearbook is about to be out, and we can invite classmates to write us messages. Um... I'm not really familiar with others at school, so can you write something for me?

t_ch: （写下严肃的寄语）
t_en: (Write a serious message)

t_ch: （写下幽默的寄语）
t_en: (Write a witty message)

t_ch: （写下煽情的寄语）
t_en: (Write an emotional message)

t_ch: 谢谢…对了，请您也保存一份吧，不管以后大家还能不能聚在一起，就当是…我们乐队的回忆。
t_en: Thank you... Oh yes, please save a copy for yourself too. No matter whether everyone stays in touch in the future or not, this will be... our band's memory.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 是…什么事呢？
t_en: What... is it?

t_ch: 你还会和乐队成员联系吗？
t_en: Will you still keep in contact with the other band members?

t_ch: 嗯，我在学校里没什么朋友，现在联系最频繁的，也就是契波同学和艾尔菲同学。我们经常聚在一起，聊聊学校的事，还有未来的打算。
t_en: Mm, I don't really have a lot of friends at school. The ones I talk to the most are Czipo and Arfey. We usually hang out together to talk about school stuff and future plans.

t_ch: 虽然大家的想法各不相同，但关系却相当亲密。就像乱破忍首所说的——「缭乱」并非是暂时组成的乐队，更是我们共同的信念与归属。
t_en: Everyone's got different ideas, but we're all pretty close. Just like Head Ninja Rappa said — The Dazzling isn't just a temporarily formed band, but a belief and sense of belonging that we share.

t_ch: 我很高兴…能在最后的校园时光里遇到经理、乱破忍首，还有乐队的两位同伴。谢谢你们。
t_en: I'm very glad... that I met you, Head Ninja Rappa, and my two bandmates during the final moments of my school time. Thank you all.

t_ch: 筑梦学院的事处理好了吗？
t_en: Has the Dreamweaver Academy matter been settled?

t_ch: 学院代表给了那位同学足够的补偿，他对乐队活动原本也没多少兴趣，所以很快就解决了，但…关于*绩点优先*原则，学生内部出现了不少反对的声音。
t_en: The academy representative gave that student sufficient compensation. He wasn't all that interested in band activities anyway, so it was settled very quickly, but... students have started to protest about the grades first principle.

t_ch: 强行将学生推到不合适，或是不感兴趣的位置上，这样的做法虽然公平，但真的是最优解吗？我想，学院代表也需要思考这个问题。
t_en: Forcing students into positions they are unsuited or uninterested in may be in the spirit of fairness, but is it really the best solution? I think the academy representative should also think this over.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 嗯。经理想聊天的时候，随时找我就好。
t_en: Sure. You can look for me any time you wish to chat.

t_ch: 好好享受这场音乐派对吧！
t_en: Enjoy this music party to the fullest!

t_ch: 我…不太喜欢人多的地方，呆在这会更轻松。经理不用管我，去忙自己的事吧。
t_en: I... don't really like crowded places. It's much more calming to stay here. Don't worry about me, Manager. You've got stuff to do.

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏碰触达凡绮的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，听到的都是相同的话语……
t_en: You try using clockwork to get in touch with Dafanchy's heart, but no matter how much the clock turns, you hear the same old words...

t_ch: 「——我等乃是『缭乱』！！！」
t_en: "...We are The Dazzling!!!"

t_ch: 看来先前的演出经历在乐队成员心中留下了极为深刻的印象，以至于任何情绪都无法动摇这份热情。这就是音乐的力量吧。
t_en: Looks like the performances left an indelible, lasting impression in the band's hearts — no amount of emotion can cause this passion to waver. This is the power of music.

t_ch: 属于我的音乐派对已经结束了，现在该专心准备毕业的相关事项。
t_en: My music party has already ended. It's now time to focus on the affairs concerning graduation.

t_ch: 但…在房间里看到那把吉他的时候，心里还是会不平静。当时那么用力地在舞台上呼喊过，这样重要的回忆…没法轻易忘记。
t_en: But... when I see that guitar in the room, my heart can't stay calm. That time when I yelled my lungs out on the stage, this important memory... is not so easily forgotten.

t_ch: 你很喜欢那三场演出吗？
t_en: You really liked those three performances?

t_ch: 是的。现在我可以毫不迟疑地说，我喜欢乱破同学的音乐，也很享受和各位成员共同组乐队的时光。这将是我在折纸大学最难忘的经历。
t_en: I did. Now I can confidently say that I like Rappa's music, and I really enjoyed the times I shared with each and every bandmate. This will be the most unforgettable experience I've had at Paperfold University.

t_ch: 当然，必须要感谢经理的帮助——如果那个时候你没有来劝我，或许我早就离开了乐队，此刻正在学院的教室里后悔吧。
t_en: Of course, this is all thanks to you, Manager — if you hadn't persuaded me back then, I might've left the band already. I'd be sulking in regret back at the classroom right now.

t_ch: …忍者，还有稀奇古怪的忍术，呵，谁能想到我这种只在乎音乐、活在闭塞世界里的无聊的人，会和这些闻所未闻的事扯上关系呢。
t_en: ...Ninja with strange bizarre ninjutsu. Heh, who would've thought someone like me — who only cares about music and lives in my own little world — would be involved in all these unheard-of things?

t_ch: 那个时候究竟发生了什么？
t_en: What exactly happened then?

t_ch: 第二场演出开始前，我返回谐乐学院，想要邀请更多老师和同学来观看我们的现场，希望「缭乱」的音乐能稍微改变大家的观念……
t_en: Before the second show started, I returned to Charmony Academy to invite more teachers and students to see our show, hoping that The Dazzling's music would change everyone's opinions, even in the slightest...

t_ch: 但那两位学院代表将我叫到了办公室，那里连通着某个小型梦境。我被关在空无一人的梦境里，责令反思自己的过错，直至乱破同学来到。
t_en: But those two academy representatives summoned me into their office, which was connected to some mini dreamscape. I was imprisoned in a deserted dreamscape to reflect on my wrongdoings until Rappa came along.

t_ch: 呵…乱破同学很早就说过，校园并非如我想象得那般平静，可那时候我以为她只是在胡言乱语，甚至还为此指责你们…对不起。
t_en: Heh... Rappa said it earlier before: The school isn't as peaceful as I thought, but I thought she was just spouting rubbish and even blamed you all for that... I'm sorry.

t_ch: 我们是为你好啊！
t_en: It was for your own good!

t_ch: 嗯，我知道…所以，这才更让我自责。
t_en: Yes, I know... That's why I am filled with self-reproach.

t_ch: 乱破不会在意的。
t_en: Rappa won't mind.

t_ch: 嗯，我知道。可惜到最后，我都没能亲口向她道歉。
t_en: Yes, I know. It's a pity that, even in the end, I didn't get a chance to personally apologize to her.

t_ch: 那你后续有什么打算？
t_en: So what's next for you?

t_ch: 我始终以音乐作为人生的目标，这点不会改变。但现在，比起在学院里接受训练，我想去接触更丰富的音乐形式，拓展自己的眼界。
t_en: I have always made music my life's goal. That much hasn't changed. But now, rather than practice at the academy, I'd rather expose myself to an even wider variety of music forms and broaden my horizons.

t_ch: 从谐乐学院毕业后，我准备去艾普瑟隆进修，同时积累演出的经验，虽然那里很难称得上是艺术的殿堂，但……
t_en: After graduating from Charmony Academy, I will prepare to further my studies at Epsilon and accrue more performance experience. That place is a far cry from what one considers a haven fit for fine arts, but...

t_ch: 要是演奏的音乐没法让观众开心，没法让自己和同伴开心，不是就没意义了吗？
t_en: If the music performance can't evoke joy in its audience, yourself, or your companions, wouldn't that be meaningless?

t_ch: 打算彻底改变音乐风格了？
t_en: You're deciding to totally change up your music style?

t_ch: 倒也不算吧。作为创作者，我们本来就不该被风格局限。风格是作品的特征，是艺术表达的惯性，但绝对不是必须达到的要求。
t_en: Not entirely. As creators, we shouldn't be confined to any style. It is a characteristic of our work, a form of artistic expression, but never a requirement that needs to be achieved.

t_ch: 就像…「旋律是对世界的记录」，经理不是也能从各种各样的奇特现象中，得到作曲灵感吗。既然你和乱破同学能做到，我也想要尝试。
t_en: Just like... "melodies are records of the world." Don't you obtain musical inspiration from all sorts of wondrous phenomena? If you and Rappa can do it, I'd like to give it a try too.

t_ch: 那个地方不太适合你…
t_en: That place doesn't really suit you...

t_ch: 我在加入「缭乱」前，也从来没想到过这样的装扮会适合自己。
t_en: Before I joined The Dazzling, I also never thought such an attire would suit me either.

t_ch: 但就算不适合，我也可以主动去适应。吸收不同的风格，学习不熟悉的知识，为未来的创作积累素材，这样才真正算是*以音乐为目标*吧。
t_en: Even if it doesn't suit me, I can also try to adapt to it. To absorb different styles, learn unfamiliar knowledge, and accumulate materials for future creations — this should be the true goal of pursuing music.

t_ch: 期待你的后续演出！
t_en: Looking forward to your future performances!

t_ch: 谢谢。要是有机会，我会邀请经理、还有乐队的各位成员来观看的。
t_en: Thank you. If I get the chance, I will invite you and the bandmates to watch me.

t_ch: …不知道乱破同学会不会感兴趣。
t_en: ...I don't know if Rappa will be interested.

t_ch: 不记得有没有和经理聊过，我的家庭管教非常严格，父母希望我把全部心思都放在学业上，将来成为音乐家，为此对我的生活做了许多限制。
t_en: I forgot if I mentioned before that my family is very strict. My parents hope I will wholly focus on my studies and become a musical maestro in the future, which is why they imposed so many restrictions on my life.

t_ch: 我…不想走他们规定的道路，我有自己对世界、对未来的理解和感悟。
t_en: I... don't want to walk on the path they dictated for me. I have my own views and perceptions of the future.

t_ch: 所以，我也需要践行*我的忍道*。
t_en: And so, I also need to follow my way of the ninja.

t_ch: 属于我的音乐派对已经结束了，现在该专心准备毕业的相关事项。
t_en: My music party has already ended. It's now time to focus on the affairs concerning graduation.

t_ch: 但…在房间里看到那把吉他的时候，心里还是会不平静。当时那么用力地在舞台上呼喊过，这样重要的回忆…没法轻易忘记。
t_en: But... when I see that guitar in the room, my heart can't stay calm. That time when I yelled my lungs out on the stage, this important memory... is not so easily forgotten.

t_ch: 你有事情要找我吗？
t_en: Do you need me for something?

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我有件事想请你帮忙。学校要制作毕业年鉴，每个学生都有自己的页面。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager, I have a favor to ask of you. The school is about to launch its graduation yearbook, and every student has their own page.

t_ch: 我…我想拜托你给我写一段赠言，毕竟…乐队的共同回忆，是任何同学都无法替代的，所以想麻烦你。不知道你现在方便吗？
t_en: I... I'd like to ask you to write a message, since... those shared memories with the band are irreplaceable, so I hope it's not too much trouble. I wonder if you're free?

t_ch: （写下严肃的寄语）
t_en: (Write a serious message)

t_ch: （写下幽默的寄语）
t_en: (Write a witty message)

t_ch: （写下煽情的寄语）
t_en: (Write an emotional message)

t_ch: 谢谢经理，我再复制一份给你，就当作是纪念了。毕业以后，希望我们都不要忘记这段为了音乐梦想而共同努力的经历。
t_en: Thank you, manager. I will make a copy for you as a souvenir. After I graduate, I hope you all won't forget this time when we all worked together for our dream of music.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 嗯，经理想找我聊些什么呢？
t_en: Mm, what do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 你还会和乐队成员联系吗？
t_en: Will you still keep in contact with the other band members?

t_ch: 我们经常会见面。尤其是财富学院的契波同学，对组织聚会特别有兴趣，时不时就要拉我们出来玩，我和达凡绮也不会拒绝就是。
t_en: We see each other all the time, especially Czipo from Fortune Academy — he's really interested in organizing meetups, dragging us out for hangouts from time to time. Dafanchy and I won't refuse anyway.

t_ch: 大家聚在一起，随便聊聊学校里发生的事，还有各自毕业后的计划。尽管来自不同学院，但彼此间的联系已经相当紧密了。
t_en: When we're together, we just talk about what happened at school and our various post-graduation plans. Even if we're from different academies, our interactions with one another are still quite frequent.

t_ch: 「缭乱」不会解散——我们都将沿各自的道路继续前行。
t_en: The Dazzling will not disband — we will all keep treading on our own paths.

t_ch: 和谐乐学院的关系还好吗？
t_en: How's your relationship with Charmony Academy?

t_ch: …学院代表很诚恳地向我道过歉，我也相信他并非是出于私心。「缭乱」的音乐确实不符合学院推崇的规范，他会做出这样的举动…我能理解
t_en: ...The academy representative earnestly apologized to me, and I'm sure he wasn't acting out of personal interest. The Dazzling's music flouted the academy's recommended regulations, resulting in his actions... I can understand that.

t_ch: 希望谐乐学院未来能接受更多样的风格和艺术形式，别局限在狭隘的视野里了。但对那群老教授而言，做到这点恐怕相当不容易吧。
t_en: I hope Charmony Academy will be more accepting of other styles and artistic forms in the future, and not be restricted by the narrow walls of their vision. But it's probably not that easily accomplished for those old professors.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: There's nothing else I want to ask.

t_ch: 好的。要是经理还有事，可以随时找我。
t_en: Okay. Do look for me if you need anything.

t_ch: 好好享受这场音乐派对吧！
t_en: Enjoy this music party to the fullest!

t_ch: 是啊，沉浸在回忆里没什么意思，也祝经理玩得开心！
t_en: Yes, it's no fun being submerged in memories. I also wish you a good time!

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏碰触艾尔菲的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，听到的都是相同的话语……
t_en: You try using clockwork to get in touch with Arfey's heart, but no matter how much the clock turns, you hear the same old words...

t_ch: 「——我等乃是『缭乱』！！！」
t_en: "...We are The Dazzling!!!"

t_ch: 看来先前的演出经历在乐队成员心中留下了极为深刻的印象，以至于任何情绪都无法动摇这份热情。这就是音乐的力量吧。
t_en: Looks like the performances left an indelible, lasting impression in the band's hearts — no amount of emotion can cause this passion to waver. This is the power of music.

t_ch: 你好，演出乐队的经理。音乐派对能取得如此成功，在银河间引起广泛关注，你和那位乐队核心的确功不可没。
t_en: Hello, band manager. The music party's success and its amassed following across the cosmos are all thanks to you and your band's lead.

t_ch: 我谨代表筑梦学院，对你们付出的努力表示感谢。
t_en: On behalf of Dreamweaver Academy, I extend my gratitude for all the work you've put in.

t_ch: 都是我应该做的。
t_en: I was just doing my job.

t_ch: 否定：作为匹诺康尼的股东，你愿意参与到学生活动当中，帮助折纸大学举办这场彩梦校庆，自然值得郑重道谢。
t_en: Negative: As Penacony's shareholder, you were willing to participate in the school's activities and help Paperfold University organize this exciting celebration. Profuse appreciation is naturally deserved.

t_ch: 只有感谢而已吗？
t_en: Just appreciation?

t_ch: 遗憾：筑梦学院无权为你们提供实质奖励。如有需要，可以向苜蓿草家系提出申请，相信他们愿意为那位代表的无礼行为表达歉意。
t_en: Remorse: Dreamweaver Academy has no authority to provide you with physical rewards. If you need anything, you can submit a request to the Alfalfa Family — I trust they will be willing to express their apology for that representative's boorish behavior.

t_ch: 至于达凡绮，自从她与乐队的各位成员接触后，性情变得愈发开朗，也在学院内建立起了良好的社交关系，不再逃避与同学的交往了。
t_en: As for Dafanchy, ever since she befriended everyone in the band, her personality has become much more jovial, and she's forged healthy social relationships within the academy. She no longer avoids socializing with her classmates.

t_ch: 相信对她而言，参加此次演出将会是段颇有纪念意义的经历。
t_en: I'm sure that participating in this show will be a memorable experience for her.

t_ch: 学习成绩有提高吗？
t_en: Did her results get better?

t_ch: 没有。相反，由于乐队排练占用了部分精力，她的成绩比以往更差了。
t_en: No. On the contrary, since band practice took up part of her attention, her grades dropped even more.

t_ch: 找到正式工作了吗？
t_en: Did she find a job?

t_ch: 没有。乐队演出经验与她的专业方向并不相关，对求职而言毫无帮助。
t_en: No. The band's performance isn't relevant to her major — it doesn't help her employment prospects at all.

t_ch: 毕业出路有着落了吗？
t_en: Did she figure out what she wants to do after graduation?

t_ch: 没有。不仅如此，结业日期临近，我却还未收到她的答辩论文，实在令人担忧。
t_en: No. Not just that, as the deadline closes near, I still haven't received her dissertation. It's simply worrying.

t_ch: 能有所成长就是好事。
t_en: It's good enough as long as she grows.

t_ch: 那是自然。作为学院代表，我也很高兴看到学员能变得更加优秀，找到自己想走的道路。再次向你和那位乐队核心表示感谢。
t_en: Naturally. As an academy representative, I am also happy to see a student growing with excellence and figuring out their own path. I have you and your band's lead to thank.

t_ch: 我有些事情想问。
t_en: I have some things to ask.

t_ch: 如果仍有疑问，请直接提出，我会尽可能为你解答。
t_en: If you still have any questions, please do ask. I will answer them as best as I can.

t_ch: 关于财富学院代表…
t_en: About the Fortune Academy representative...

t_ch: 正如知更鸟小姐所说，苜蓿草家系撤销了他的学院代表职务。他的渎职行为对学校声誉造成了极大影响，不可能继续在校内任职。
t_en: It is just as Miss Robin said, the Alfalfa Family has rescinded his academy representative role. His irresponsible behavior has greatly sullied the academy's repute, and will no longer be able to hold his post.

t_ch: 学生们原本便对这位代表颇有微词，校方也曾收到过相关投诉，只是碍于苜蓿草家系的情面，才始终没有处理。
t_en: There have been students who have been critical of this representative, and the school has also received related complaints. It was due to the considerations of the Alfalfa Family that this problem was never resolved.

t_ch: 相信此次事件过后，他会得到应有的惩处。
t_en: With the passing of this matter, I believe that he will receive just punishment.

t_ch: 关于谐乐学院代表…
t_en: About the Charmony Academy representative...

t_ch: 我与那位代表相识很长时间了，知道他是位真心替学生着想，将艺术理念看得比名誉更为重要的优秀教师。
t_en: I've known that rep for a long time, and I know he sincerely cares for the students. He is an excellent teacher who puts artistic ideals far above fame.

t_ch: 但…正是这份对艺术的执着，才驱使他做出了阻挠乐队登台的行为。学院的训练既是知识，同样是对我们视野的限制。
t_en: But... it is that obstinacy with art that drove him to do what he did to hinder the band's performance. Knowledge is what students learn in school, but it is also what limits our horizons.

t_ch: 或许我也该多听听学生们的想法，避免陷入固步自封的境地。
t_en: Perhaps I should pay more attention to what the students say, so we don't get entrapped in our own hubris.

t_ch: 我没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 若是还有疑问，你可以随时与我沟通。
t_en: If you still have any further questions, please talk to me anytime.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 美梦多彩，理想自由——折纸大学的校训始终如此，作为学院代表，我们理应做出表率，将这种精神贯彻下去。
t_en: Color your dreams, free your ideals — this has always been Paperfold University's motto. As a representative of the academy, we should lead by example and uphold this spirit.

t_ch: 希望你能和折纸大学的全体师生一起，共同享受这场音乐派对。
t_en: I hope you, along with all Paperfold University's teachers, will share in this music party's joy.

t_ch: 美梦多彩，理想自由——折纸大学的校训始终如此，作为学院代表，我们理应做出表率，将这种精神贯彻下去。
t_en: Color your dreams, free your ideals — this has always been Paperfold University's motto. As a representative of the academy, we should lead by example and uphold this spirit.

t_ch: 音乐派对能取得如此成功，在银河间引起广泛关注，你和那位乐队核心的确功不可没。
t_en: The music party's success and amassed following across the cosmos are all thanks to you and the core members of your band.

t_ch: 我谨代表筑梦学院，对你们付出的努力表示感谢。
t_en: On behalf of Dreamweaver Academy, I extend my gratitude for all the work you've put in.

t_ch: 无需任何质疑，以绩点为评判依据才是最公平有效的考核方式。
t_en: There is no debate. Using grades as the basis of evaluation is the fairest and most effective assessment method.

t_ch: 我早就说过，无论是才能还是效益都不可靠，绩点…只有绩点才是最正确的。
t_en: I've said it before. Neither talent nor efficacy is reliable. Grades... only grades are most correct.

t_ch: 幽默：看看那两个学院，坚持才能优先的，最后受限于自己的认知，做出错误的判断。宣称效益优先的，其实想的都是自己的利益。
t_en: Humor: Look at those two academies. The first champions aptitude above all else, yet ironically finds itself shackled by its own narrow perspectives, leading to flawed decisions. The second proclaims efficiency as its guiding principle, but in reality, merely pursues its own self-interest.

t_ch: 为什么？因为这两个衡量标准都是假的，没法用数字量化，而数字不会骗人。
t_en: Why? Because these two standards of measurement are unquantifiable and therefore false. Numbers do not lie.

t_ch: 谁能断言自己比其余人更有天赋？谁能说清自己的提议能带来多少效益？模糊的标准只会带来争端，最终导向错误与偏见。
t_en: Who can claim themselves more talented than others? Who can justify that their own suggestions bring about the greatest efficiency? Ambiguous standards only fuel dispute, resulting in mistaken trajectories and bias.

t_ch: 那达凡绮该怎么解释呢？
t_en: How would you explain Dafanchy then?

t_ch: 我承认，这是我的个人判断。这种情况不应再次出现。对，不符合原则。
t_en: I admit, that was my personal judgment. Such instances shouldn't happen again. Yes, it is misaligned with the principle.

t_ch: 但…绩点无法规定学员的未来，作为学院代表，我必须顾虑更多。
t_en: But... grades can't dictate a student's future. As the academy representative, I must consider this carefully.

t_ch: 督促学生认真学习，这不是教师的职责么…我做错了吗？
t_en: To guide a student's diligent study... Is that not a teacher's duty...? Have I done something wrong?

t_ch: 你知道吗，学员中流传着一份名为「我最讨厌的老师」的共享文档。
t_en: Do you know that there is a shared file named "Teachers I Hate Most" that's making its rounds among students?

t_ch: 在其中，排名第一的是那位财富学院代表，而我居然在第二位。真是不可思议。
t_en: Inside, the top-ranked teacher is that Fortune Academy representative, and I am surprisingly just below him. It was simply astonishing.

t_ch: 难道是因为我平时对待他们过分严格？但我这样做，都是希望他们能将心思放在学业上。督促学员认真学习，这不是教师的职责么？
t_en: Is it because I'm usually too strict with them? But I do this in the hopes that they will concentrate on their studies. To guide a student's diligent study, is that not a teacher's duty?

t_ch: 悲伤：遭到学员误解，或许是所有教师的宿命。
t_en: Sorrow: Being misunderstood by students is perhaps just the wretched life of all teachers.

t_ch: …罢了，忧虑这种问题并无益处。这个给你，权当作为听我发牢骚的歉意。
t_en: ...Forget it. It's meaningless to mull over such problems. This is for you. Just take it as an apology for my grumblings.

t_ch: …你知道吗，学员中流传着一份名为「我最讨厌的老师」的共享文档。
t_en: ...Do you know that there is a shared file named "Teachers I Hate Most" that's making its rounds among students?

t_ch: 在其中，排名第一的是那位财富学院代表，而我居然在第二位。真是不可思议。
t_en: Inside, the top-ranked teacher is that Fortune Academy representative, and I am surprisingly just below him. It was simply astonishing.

t_ch: 难道是因为我平时对待他们过分严格？但我这样做，都是希望他们能将心思放在学业上。督促学员认真学习，这不是教师的职责么？
t_en: Is it because I'm usually too strict with them? But I do this in the hopes that they will concentrate on their studies. To guide a student's diligent study, is that not a teacher's duty?

t_ch: 悲伤：遭到学员误解，或许是所有教师的宿命。
t_en: Sorrow: Being misunderstood by students is perhaps just the wretched life of all teachers.

t_ch: 我要求取消财富学院的独立地位，划归到筑梦学院管辖内！
t_en: I request that Fortune Academy's independence be revoked, and returned under the oversight of the Dreamweaver Academy!

t_ch: 我厌恶财富学院的那群人很久了！
t_en: I've been tired of those Fortune Academy snobs for far too long!

t_ch: 占用学校最多的资源，教学活动的经费也最为充足，结果无论是发表的论文数，还是学生就业率都远远比不上我们筑梦学院。
t_en: They hog most of the school's resources, and they always have ample teaching event budget. And even then, their number of published papers and student employment rates far pale in comparison to the Dreamweaver Academy's.

t_ch: 预算如此充分，却始终毫无建树，绝对是被那几个不学无术的负责人挥霍掉了！
t_en: Equipped with such a generous budget, but without any accolades to show for it — a clear sign of squandered opportunities by incompetent leaders!

t_ch: 质问：你如何看待现今的学院规划？
t_en: Query: How do you view the current academy plan?

t_ch: 确实，我赞成你的看法。
t_en: Honestly, I fully agree with you.

t_ch: 请求：作为匹诺康尼的股东，你可以和我联名提出申请，要求取消财富学院的独立地位，划归到筑梦学院管辖内。
t_en: Request: As Penacony's shareholder, you can submit a joint proposal with me to revoke Fortune Academy's independence and place it under the jurisdiction of Dreamweaver Academy.

t_ch: 梦境才是匹诺康尼的根基，没有稳固的建筑，所有财富都是空谈！
t_en: The dreamscapes are Penacony's foundation. Without structural stability, all the wealth in the world is nothing but empty talk!

t_ch: 我不同意你的观点。
t_en: I disagree with your perspective.

t_ch: 是吗？但我阐述的都是事实，数据清晰，逻辑完整。更何况，那位代表的所作所为你都见到了，完全不符合教师的规范。
t_en: Do you? But I expound the truth — the numbers are clear, and the logic sound. You have also seen that representative's actions, which have made a farce of teaching standards.

t_ch: 尝试：我可以向学校提出申请，要求取消财富学院的独立地位，划归到筑梦学院管辖内。希望这份提案能够通过。
t_en: Attempt: I can submit a proposal to the school to revoke Fortune Academy's independence and bring it under the jurisdiction of Dreamweaver Academy. I hope this proposal will be approved.

t_ch: 哼，做得不错啊。没看出来你还挺有当经理的天赋的，匹诺康尼的大股东！
t_en: Hah, well done. I never took you for someone with a managerial knack, major shareholder of Penacony.

t_ch: 那当然！
t_en: And I'm pretty good at it!

t_ch: 别太得意忘形。艺术是条看不见尽头的路，走的越远，才越会觉得慌张。要是以为自己现在很厉害，就大错特错了！
t_en: Don't get full of yourself. Art is a road with no finish line — The further you go, the more unsure you will be. You'd be gravely mistaken if you think you're already good enough!

t_ch: 过奖，只是小股东罢了。
t_en: You're too kind. I'm just a minor shareholder.

t_ch: 有区别吗？都是提需求的大人物，都是学校的贵宾，我可惹不起！
t_en: Does it make a difference? All of you are important figures making proposals and VIPs of the school. I can't afford to offend any of you!

t_ch: …跟你在这较劲也没什么意思。演出都结束了，从结果来看，咳…你们也算完成了任务，虽然我是不明白这种音乐到底哪点好了。
t_en: ...It's pointless to argue with you. The show is over, and from the outcome, ahem... I suppose you've all completed the assignment, though I still don't get what's so good about this music.

t_ch: 但既然学生都喜欢，观众也表示认可，那行吧，姑且表扬下你们也不是不可以。
t_en: But if the students like it and the audience also approves, then that's fine. I'm not above giving you a compliment or two.

t_ch: 老师，时代变了。
t_en: Teacher, times have changed.

t_ch: 是啊，时代是变了，我熟悉的音乐风格渐渐都见不到了，而我擅长的那些技法和规范，也成了你们口中老旧的学院派。
t_en: Yes, times have changed. The music styles I'm familiar with have slowly become irrelevant, and the techniques and standards I pride myself in are now also the dated concepts students gripe about.

t_ch: 但是…音乐的理念、艺术的精神，还有我的坚持都是不会变的！
t_en: But... the ideas about music, the spirit of art, and my resolution will never waver!

t_ch: 别勉强自己。
t_en: Don't be too hard on yourself.

t_ch: 哪有勉强！我是那种冥顽不化、不懂得变通的老师吗？我也是很擅长发现学生优点的，只不过…哼，你们的音乐确实和我想得不一样。
t_en: Too hard? Never! Do I look like the sort of teacher who's obstinate and unable to adapt? I'm also good at discovering students' strengths. It's just... hmph, your kind of music does deviate from what I had in mind.

t_ch: 好了好了，去玩你的吧。随便到哪里转转，都比和我这种老家伙废话要强！
t_en: Alright, alright, you go and play elsewhere. Going anywhere else would be better than engaging in pointless banter with this old coot!

t_ch: 我有些事情想问。
t_en: I have some things to ask.

t_ch: 那就快点说，我还赶着去看学生的表演呢。
t_en: Make it quick then. I'm in a hurry to watch a student performance.

t_ch: 关于财富学院代表…
t_en: About the Fortune Academy representative...

t_ch: 先前说过，我是被那个坏家伙给骗了！他用艾尔菲的前途来威胁我，我…我一时没想清楚，就答应了他的计划。
t_en: I've already told you that I was lied to by that baddie! He threatened me with Arfey's future! I... I wasn't thinking straight and agreed to his plan.

t_ch: 这你可得相信我，哪有老师会害自己的学生？我是真心实意为艾尔菲着想，要是继续跟你们胡闹，谁知道他还会使出什么手段？
t_en: You have to believe me on this. Which teacher would wish harm on his students? I really was trying to look out for Arfey. If I continued to make a ruckus with you guys, who knows what horrors he could have committed?

t_ch: 哎…现在说这个也什么用，是我判断失误了，我…对不起你们。
t_en: *sigh* ...It's pointless to say anything of this now. It was an error on my part. I... I have wronged all of you.

t_ch: 关于筑梦学院代表…
t_en: About Dreamweaver Academy representative...

t_ch: 说句实话，筑梦学院就不该参与到这件事里来。街头的自动乐团，懂什么是音乐吗？知道什么叫做*触动心灵*吗？
t_en: To be honest, Dreamweaver Academy shouldn't have even been involved in this. What would street automated orchestras know about music? Can they even comprehend the concept of music that touches the soul?

t_ch: 旋律可不是什么乐器振动，而是灵魂的碰撞！让那个代表来讨论灵魂，就像把美梦剧团当成人那样不可理喻！
t_en: Melodies aren't just instruments vibrating. They are collisions among souls! Getting that representative to discuss the soul is as absurd as treating the Sweet Dreams Troupe as human beings!

t_ch: 哼，要是还有下次，我会向学校申请的。和音乐相关的事务，只能由谐乐学院决定，其余学院都不行。
t_en: Hmph, if there comes a next time, I will make a request to the school — That all affairs regarding music will only be decided by Charmony Academy. The other academies don't cut it.

t_ch: 我没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 就这样吧，没事别来打扰我。
t_en: We're done here. Don't bother me unless it's important.
